Off Calendar Jun-09-2014 Continued To Aug-27-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-14-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: FREDERICK SHEINFIELD, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3800, FOR DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling re: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer as to the first, fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action sustained without leave to amend. The allegations in the First Amended Complaint fail to establish that Defendant is a "person" subject to liability under either Bus. & Prof. Code section 17200 et seq. or Labor Code section 2056. Moreover, as a government entity, Defendant can only be held liable as provided by statute (Govt. Code 815(b)), and Plaintiff failed to provide any statutory basis for liability for the common law torts of intentional and negligent interference with prospective economic advantage. In addition, for liability to attach to a government entity under Govt. Code section 815, the statute creating the mandatory duty must intend to protect against the kind of risk of injury suffered by the party asserting section 815.6 as a basis for liability. (California v. Sup. Ct. (Perry) (1984) 150 Cal.App. 3d 848, 854.) Here, the injury that Labor Code section 4616 is designed to forestall is an injury to employees, not to providers. Thus, there is no liability for the City for its alleged failure to comply with Labor Code section 4616. Demurrer to the second cause of action is moot pursuant to entry of dismissal of this cause of action as against Defendant. Demurrer to the third cause of action is granted with leave to amend in order for Plaintiff to allege facts specifying which conduct on behalf of Defendant constituted the deprivation of due process. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sandy Carranza #7062.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
